title: Verilator outputs incorrect result for conditional ternary expression involving bit-slice and shift

features.name: ternary operator with bit-slice shift in false branch
features.description: Code should include a ternary operator where the false branch performs a bit-slice operation followed by a right shift with 8-bit literal shift amount
features.snippet: logic [7:0] in4; wire [0:0] result; assign result = (1'b0) ? 1'b1 : (in4[18:18] >> 8'b0001);

features.name: bit-slice on non-zero base index input signal
features.description: Code should include a bit-slice operation on an input signal declared with non-zero starting index (e.g., input [23:16])
features.snippet: input wire [23:16] in4; wire bit_val; assign bit_val = in4[18:18];

features.name: modulo operator as falsy ternary condition
features.description: Code should include a modulo expression used as the condition in a ternary operator that evaluates to a falsy (zero) value
features.snippet: logic [7:0] in3; wire condition; assign condition = (in3 % 4'o16); wire sel = condition ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;

features.name: replication operator with single-bit pattern
features.description: Code should include a replication operator with a single-bit pattern used in the true branch of a ternary expression
